concerns.text: SC-002 states 'sufficient observations per dataset' without threshold. Idea requires 1000+ observations for statistical power at 1-5% anomaly rates. Spec must explicitly state '≥1000 observations' to match data-model.md schema constraint.

Spec mentions 'Total Files: 8 (6 schema tests + 2 service interface tests)' but plan.md includes 9 schema files with streaming_dpgmm.schema.yaml. Missing streaming_dpgmm schema story creates verification gap.

Idea specifies 'paired t-test on F1-scores across datasets, with Bonferroni correction for multiple comparisons' but spec lacks explicit statistical comparison requirements. This is core research methodology missing from spec.

Idea requires 'update posterior after each new observation in streaming mode' but AnomalyDetectorService lacks explicit streaming posterior update method documentation. update_model() exists but streaming semantics unclear.
Idea specifies memory <7GB RAM and runtime <30 min per dataset for GHA compatibility. Spec SC-001 through SC-006 omit these critical resource constraints that were explicitly in methodology sketch.
Dataset schema defines 'timestamp' (singular) but time_series_dataset.schema.yaml defines 'timestamps' (plural). Field naming inconsistency between data model and contracts creates ambiguity.
Quickstart references 'electricity_labels.csv' but idea states labels come from synthetic anomaly injection, not external CSV. This contradicts the research methodology and creates false dependency.
